source.title: Flash Fire and Explosion at Sunoco Partners Nederland Terminal

event.incident_type: Hot work incident involving explosive atmosphere in piping
actions_taken: Emergency responders (Jefferson County Sheriff's Office, Nederland Fire Department, EMS, Air Rescue) dispatched at 8:40 PM
Triage area established by fire department
Four injured workers transported via helicopter and ambulance to hospital
Three injured workers treated for minor injuries and released
OSHA citations issued to L-Con in February 2017
OSHA serious citation issued to CARBER in February 2017
PHMSA cited Sunoco for failure to report the incident
event.summary: On August 12, 2016, at approximately 8:15 PM CDT, a flash fire and explosion occurred while L-Con contractor was welding a piping segment at the Sunoco Nederland crude oil terminal. The 30-inch diameter piping section contained residual crude oil and was isolated using CARBER isolation tools. During internal welding of flange A, vapor inside the piping ignited, causing a pressure spike that ejected the isolation tools from both ends of the pipe. The explosion and flash fire injured seven contractors (four welders, one fire watch, two others), with four requiring hospitalization for burns, bone fractures, and blast-related injuries. The incident was caused by hot work being conducted on equipment containing an explosive atmosphere without proper clearing or inerting of flammable material.
recommendations: Equipment containing flammable materials must be purged, made safe, or decontaminated prior to hot work
Hot work activities must not proceed if flammable vapors are present above 10% LEL
Continuous atmospheric monitoring for flammable gases is preferred during hot work
Alternative precautions such as inert gas purging must be established when piping cannot be fully decontaminated
Isolation devices must be continuously monitored until servicing work is completed
Formal JHA must be performed each time a piping mechanical seal device is applied
Mechanical seal devices should only be used after available means of vapor removal have been employed

hazards.name: Flammable crude oil vapors in piping
Residual liquid crude oil in piping
Ignition sources from hot work (welding, grinding)

threats.name: Failure to clear or inert piping of flammable vapors prior to hot work
Isolation tool seal failure or degradation
Ineffective implementation of hot work procedures

consequences.name: Flash fire and explosion in piping
Ejection of isolation tools and crude oil from pipe ends

controls.name: Depressurization and draining of piping
controls.barrier_role: Remove liquid hydrocarbon from piping to reduce fuel source
controls.lod_basis: OSHA and industry standards require depressurization and draining prior to hot work

supporting_text: Sunoco operators ceased crude oil flow and drained the piping through that piping segment
L-Con workers determined there was approximately one-and-a-half-inches to two-inches deep level of crude oil that remained
The crude oil, drained to the containment pool, was removed via vacuum truck to be reclaimed

controls.name: CARBER isolation tool installation
controls.barrier_role: Create physical barrier between residual crude oil vapors and hot work area
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical seal device designed to isolate piping for hot work by creating positive pressure vapor barrier

supporting_text: CARBER then installed their company's isolation tool to provide a barrier between the contents inside the piping segment and the external hot work activities
After approximately three to four inches of welding the inside surface of the flange, vapor inside the piping, between two of the installed CARBER isolation tools, ignited
The ignition initiated a pressure spike that, in turn, caused the isolation tools at flanges A and B to blow out of the open pipe at the north and south ends

controls.name: Atmospheric monitoring and testing
controls.barrier_role: Detect presence of flammable vapors before hot work commences
controls.lod_basis: OSHA standards require approved instrumentation to monitor flammable gases; Sunoco procedure requires continuous monitoring

supporting_text: According to the work permit issued the night of the incident, atmospheric testing was conducted at 7:00 p.m. and did not detect any flammable vapors
There were no accounts from the welders that the continuous monitor alarmed at any time during welding activities, which would be indicative of flammable vapors in the area
There were no accounts of the specifics on the exact location(s) of where atmospheric testing was conducted
A continuous monitor was mounted on the railing close to where welding was occurring

controls.name: Hot work permit issuance and approval
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper authorization and verification of safety precautions before hot work
controls.lod_basis: Sunoco Overview of Work Permits procedure requires issuance of work permits for hot work activities with specific fire protection precautions

supporting_text: A CIS employee, on behalf of Sunoco, then issued a hot work permit to L-Con, permitting them to start welding
The issued hot work permit required a fire watch, fire extinguisher, and a continuous flammable gas monitor for the job

Sunoco's Hot Work procedure specified: Equipment that has been in flammable or combustible service or may be contaminated with hazardous materials shall be purged, made safe, or decontaminated prior to conducting hot work on the equipment

controls.name: Job Hazard Analysis (JHA) completion
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and required controls for hot work activities
controls.lod_basis: L-Con's Welding and Cutting Safety procedure requires JHA for all welding and cutting operations

supporting_text: Prior to starting the hot work the day of the incident, L-Con conducted a JHA (Job Hazard Analysis)
L-Con identified a variety of hazards on the JHA checklist for the welding work planned at the Nederland terminal, including: Hot work (burns, fire, explosion)
L-Con identified 'welding sparks/ and grinding' as a hazard associated with this job task

controls.name: Fire watch personnel assignment
controls.barrier_role: Monitor for fire hazards and respond to ignition events during hot work
controls.lod_basis: Sunoco and L-Con procedures require fire watch personnel during hot work activities

supporting_text: The issued hot work permit required a fire watch, fire extinguisher, and a continuous flammable gas monitor for the job
The forced ejection of the isolation tools and the flash fire from the pipe section injured seven contractors, including four welders, one fire watch, and two other contractors

controls.name: Continuous monitoring of isolation device seal integrity
controls.barrier_role: Verify and maintain seal integrity of isolation tools throughout hot work
controls.lod_basis: CARBER procedures call for monitoring seal integrity using pressure gauge; L-Con settlement agreement required continuous monitoring

supporting_text: The Isolation Tool provides a means to monitor the seal integrity using a pressure gauge attached to the water outlet
If no leaks are found and the water pressure holds steady, the isolation tool seals are proven (or verified)

L-Con and OSHA reached a Settlement Agreement, where L-Con received one other than serious violation of 29 CFR 1910.147(d)(5)(ii) for failing to ensure its subcontractor continuously monitored the isolation devices the subcontractor installed until the servicing work was completed

controls.name: Inert gas purging of piping
controls.barrier_role: Remove flammable vapors by displacing with inert gas (nitrogen or argon)
controls.lod_basis: Sunoco and L-Con procedures specify inert gas purging as alternative when piping cannot be fully decontaminated

supporting_text: In situations where piping systems or other process equipment cannot be isolated or fully decontaminated, alternative precautions, such as purging with inert gases, must be established to ensure that welding and cutting can be conducted safely

L-Con's Welding and Cutting Safety procedure stated: Those controls may include a nitrogen or argon blanket to inert the space or a high-pressure plug to isolate the hot work from the interior of the space

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
